edges.description: ALK fusion proteins signal constitutively to STAT3.
STAT3 activity sustains anti-apoptotic transcriptional output.

edges.target: JAK/STAT3 Pathway Alteration in ALK-Negative ALCL
JAK/STAT3 Pathway Alteration in ALK-Negative ALCL

nodes.description: Systemic ALK-positive ALCL is initiated by chromosomal rearrangements that generate ALK fusion oncogenes, most commonly NPM1::ALK.

evidence.reference_title: The Pathological Spectrum of Systemic Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma (ALCL).
evidence.snippet: ALK is rearranged in approximately 80% of systemic ALCL cases with one of its partner genes, most commonly NPM1

evidence.reference_title: The Pathological Spectrum of Systemic Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma (ALCL).
evidence.snippet: ALK is rearranged in approximately 80% of systemic ALCL cases with one of its partner genes, most commonly NPM1

nodes.description: Activated ALK constitutively phosphorylates and activates STAT3 in systemic ALK-positive ALCL, establishing a central survival and growth program.

biological_processes: cell surface receptor signaling pathway via JAK-STAT
cell surface receptor protein tyrosine kinase signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) activates Stat3 and protects hematopoietic cells from cell death.
evidence.snippet: We show here that expression of activated ALK induces the constitutive phosphorylation of Stat3 in transfected cells as well as in primary human ALCLs.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Insights into the Diagnosis of Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma: Beyond Morphology and Immunophenotype.
evidence.snippet: In ALK-positive ALCL, ALK fusion proteins drive oncogenesis via constitutive activation of STAT3 and other signaling pathways.

nodes.description: Anthracycline-containing multiagent chemotherapy remains a core systemic treatment backbone for ALK-positive systemic ALCL.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma: pathology, genetics, and clinical aspects.
evidence.snippet: Patients with ALK-positive ALCL are usually treated with anthracycline-based regimens, such as combination cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisolone (CHOP) or CHOEP (CHOP plus etoposide)

nodes.description: ALK-positive ALCL uses STAT3-dependent BCL2L1 transcription to resist cell death and support clonal outgrowth.

evidence.reference_title: Anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) activates Stat3 and protects hematopoietic cells from cell death.
evidence.snippet: These studies support a pathogenic mechanism whereby stimulation of anti-apoptotic signals through activation of Stat3 contributes to the successful outgrowth of ALK positive tumor cells.

nodes.description: Frontline brentuximab vedotin plus CHP improved progression-free and overall survival over CHOP in previously untreated systemic ALCL and other CD30-positive PTCL.

evidence.reference_title: FDA Approval Summary: Brentuximab Vedotin in First-Line Treatment of Peripheral T-Cell Lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: In November 2018, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved brentuximab vedotin (BV) for the treatment of adult patients with previously untreated systemic anaplastic large cell lymphoma

evidence.reference_title: FDA Approval Summary: Brentuximab Vedotin in First-Line Treatment of Peripheral T-Cell Lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: The median PFS was 48.2 months with BV+CHP versus 20.8 months with CHOP, resulting in a hazard ratio (HR) of 0.71

nodes.description: ALK inhibition with crizotinib has meaningful activity in relapsed or refractory systemic ALK-positive ALCL.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and safety of crizotinib in ALK-positive systemic anaplastic large-cell lymphoma in children, adolescents, and adult patients: results of the French AcSé-crizotinib trial.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: Crizotinib shows efficacy and an acceptable safety profile in ALK+ ALCL relapsed/refractory patients.

nodes.description: Many ALK-negative ALCLs activate JAK/STAT signaling through JAK1 and/or STAT3 mutations instead of ALK fusion signaling. Similar lesions are also seen in breast implant-associated ALCL.
nodes.id: JAK/STAT3 Pathway Alteration in ALK-Negative ALCL

biological_processes: cell surface receptor signaling pathway via JAK-STAT

evidence.reference_title: ALK-Negative Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma: Current Concepts and Molecular Pathogenesis of a Heterogeneous Group of Large T-Cell Lymphomas.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, systemic ALK- ALCL-apart from DUSP22-rearranged cases-harbors JAK1 and/or STAT3 mutations that result in the activation of the JAK/STAT signaling pathway. The JAK1/3 and STAT3 mutations have also been identified in BIA-ALCL but not in pc-ALCL.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Insights into the Diagnosis of Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma: Beyond Morphology and Immunophenotype.
evidence.snippet: ALK-negative ALCL comprises heterogeneous genetic subtypes, in which JAK/STAT3 pathway alterations and novel gene fusions are gaining recognition as potential therapeutic targets.

evidence.reference_title: ALK-Negative Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma: Current Concepts and Molecular Pathogenesis of a Heterogeneous Group of Large T-Cell Lymphomas.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, systemic ALK- ALCL-apart from DUSP22-rearranged cases-harbors JAK1 and/or STAT3 mutations that result in the activation of the JAK/STAT signaling pathway.

nodes.description: ALK-positive ALCL can upregulate PD-L1/CD274 through STAT3, adding an immune-suppressive phenotype to the malignant T-cell program.

biological_processes: negative regulation of T cell mediated immunity

evidence.reference_title: Oncogenic kinase NPM/ALK induces through STAT3 expression of immunosuppressive protein CD274 (PD-L1, B7-H1).
evidence.snippet: NPM/ALK induces CD274 expression by activating its key signal transmitter, transcription factor STAT3.

evidence.reference_title: The potent oncogene NPM-ALK mediates malignant transformation of normal human CD4(+) T lymphocytes.
evidence.snippet: These unique features, which are strictly dependent on NPM-ALK activity and expression, include perpetual cell growth, proliferation, and survival; activation of the key signal transduction pathways STAT3 and mTORC1; and expression of CD30 (the hallmark of anaplastic large-cell lymphoma) and of immunosuppressive cytokine IL-10 and cell-surface protein PD-L1/CD274.

evidence.reference_title: ALK-Negative Anaplastic Large Cell Lymphoma: Current Concepts and Molecular Pathogenesis of a Heterogeneous Group of Large T-Cell Lymphomas.
evidence.snippet: Additionally, systemic ALK- ALCL-apart from DUSP22-rearranged cases-harbors JAK1 and/or STAT3 mutations that result in the activation of the JAK/STAT signaling pathway.

nodes.description: In relapsed or refractory systemic ALCL, single-agent brentuximab vedotin can produce durable complete remissions in a subset of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Five-year results of brentuximab vedotin in patients with relapsed or refractory systemic anaplastic large cell lymphoma.
evidence.snippet: These final results, which demonstrated a high rate of peripheral neuropathy resolution, and durable remissions in a subset of patients with relapsed or refractory systemic ALCL, provide evidence that single-agent brentuximab vedotin may be a potentially curative treatment option.
